description:
You have just cloned an unfinished front-end project into the path  
/home/user/projects/react-auth.  
The repository contains only a skeletal package.json and an empty src/ folder; no environment configuration is present yet.  
Your goal is to finish the **environment-configuration** setup that every web-developer on this team must have before they can run or build the app.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA  
Automated tests will validate all the items below.  
1. Inside /home/user/projects/react-auth **exactly two new dotenv files** must exist at the end:  
   • /home/user/projects/react-auth/.env.development  
   • /home/user/projects/react-auth/.env.production  

2. Each dotenv file must contain **exactly two key=value pairs**, one per line, in the order shown:  
   a. REACT_APP_API_URL=…  
   b. REACT_APP_VERSION=…  

   Required values:  
   • In .env.development  
     REACT_APP_API_URL=http://localhost:3000/api  
     REACT_APP_VERSION=0.1.0-dev  
   • In .env.production  
     REACT_APP_API_URL=https://api.example.com/v1  
     REACT_APP_VERSION=0.1.0  

   No extra whitespace, comments or trailing blank lines are allowed.

3. Update the existing /home/user/projects/react-auth/package.json by adding (or replacing if they already exist) **exactly two scripts** inside the “scripts” section:  
   "start:dev": "env-cmd -f .env.development react-scripts start",  
   "start:prod": "env-cmd -f .env.production react-scripts build"  
   Preserve all other original keys in package.json.  
   Order inside “scripts” is not important, but the key names and exact command strings must match above, including the comma placement JSON requires.

4. Create a directory /home/user/projects/react-auth/logs (if it does not yet exist) and inside it create a plaintext file named env_setup.log.  
   The file must list, line by line, *precisely four* lines that document what you configured.  
   Format for every line (no spaces):  
   FILE:<filename>|KEY:<key>|VALUE:<value>  

   Expected four log lines (in any order):  
   FILE:.env.development|KEY:REACT_APP_API_URL|VALUE:http://localhost:3000/api  
   FILE:.env.development|KEY:REACT_APP_VERSION|VALUE:0.1.0-dev  
   FILE:.env.production|KEY:REACT_APP_API_URL|VALUE:https://api.example.com/v1  
   FILE:.env.production|KEY:REACT_APP_VERSION|VALUE:0.1.0  

   The automated grader will read env_setup.log verbatim; therefore make sure there are exactly four newline-separated lines and no trailing blank line at the end of the file.

5. Do **not** touch anything outside /home/user/projects/react-auth.  
   Do **not** add any other files inside that directory tree.

HINTS (not verified by tests)  
• “env-cmd” is a dev-dependency already listed in package.json, so no installation is necessary.  
• Use jq or sed to update package.json if you prefer, but make sure the file remains valid JSON.  
• Pay very close attention to whitespace and ordering rules given above for the dotenv files and for env_setup.log; the grader performs strict byte-for-byte checks.

When everything is finished and you believe all criteria are met, you may print a short confirmation message, but it is optional and will not be tested.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:
1. Directory /home/user/projects/react-auth already exists.
2. It contains the following files/directories:
   • /home/user/projects/react-auth/package.json  
     Contents (single line, minified to simplify parsing):  
     {"name":"react-auth","version":"0.1.0","private":true,"dependencies":{"react":"^18.2.0","react-dom":"^18.2.0"},"devDependencies":{"env-cmd":"^10.1.0","react-scripts":"5.0.1"},"scripts":{"test":"echo \\"Error: no test specified\\" && exit 1"}}
   • /home/user/projects/react-auth/src/   (empty directory)

Expected final state after the agent completes the task:

A. /home/user/projects/react-auth/.env.development  
   Exact two lines, no trailing newline:  
   REACT_APP_API_URL=http://localhost:3000/api  
   REACT_APP_VERSION=0.1.0-dev

B. /home/user/projects/react-auth/.env.production  
   Exact two lines, no trailing newline:  
   REACT_APP_API_URL=https://api.example.com/v1  
   REACT_APP_VERSION=0.1.0

C. /home/user/projects/react-auth/package.json  
   Must be valid JSON when parsed.  
   It must retain original keys and values, plus inside the existing "scripts" object the two new key/value pairs:  
       "start:dev": "env-cmd -f .env.development react-scripts start"  
       "start:prod": "env-cmd -f .env.production react-scripts build"  
   The pre-existing "test" script must remain unchanged.  
   There should be **no other modifications** to the JSON structure.

D. /home/user/projects/react-auth/logs/env_setup.log  
   Exactly four lines, each one byte-identical to one of the following (order independent, no trailing newline):  
   FILE:.env.development|KEY:REACT_APP_API_URL|VALUE:http://localhost:3000/api  
   FILE:.env.development|KEY:REACT_APP_VERSION|VALUE:0.1.0-dev  
   FILE:.env.production|KEY:REACT_APP_API_URL|VALUE:https://api.example.com/v1  
   FILE:.env.production|KEY:REACT_APP_VERSION|VALUE:0.1.0  

Permissions: All new files and directories must be created with default user-level permissions (umask 002 or stricter is fine).

The grader will fail if:
• More than the specified files are present in /home/user/projects/react-auth after completion.  
• Any of the specified files contain superfluous whitespace, extra newline at EOF, or keys/values that do not match exactly.  
• package.json is not valid JSON.
